text: Abstract—Data science research has been undergoing numerous advancements. Abstract Big Data Analytics and Deep Learning are two hotspots of data science. Big Data is a data source that has drawn lots of attentions in the data science area recently. Most governmental organizations and commercial organizations are generating huge volumes of data on a wide range of topics and domains. Useful insights and patterns for solving real world data analysis problems such as intelligence, cybersecurity, financial fraud detection, marketing and medical informatics can be drawn out from the collected data. More than fifty percent of data analyzed by Google and Microsoft are used for business and strategic purposes, which can significantly impact current and future technological advancements. Deep Learning is a part of a class of machine learning algorithms, typically worked with a large amount of data, trained using artificial neural networks on images and uncoverable patterns. Deep Learning forms higher level abstractions from lower level abstractions of data in a hierarchical way. For example, given some abstract representation at a certain layer of a Deep Learning architecture, a model at the next layer uses it to obtain more abstract representations. Hence Deep Learning has proven that it can handle Big Data Analytics to some extent due to its ability to deal with a large amount of unlabeled and unstructured data and the ability to solve discriminative classification tasks more efficiently. The goal of this paper is to investigate Big Data Analytics and its relationship with Deep Learning by examining and exploring its ability in extracting meaningful patterns from high volumes of data, semantic indexing, data tagging, efficient information search and easy discriminative classification. Aspects of Deep Learning that need to be investigated further in order to handle challenges posed by Big Data Analytics will also be presented. Finally, this study aims at suggesting future works for Big Data Analytics and Deep Learning by presenting the solutions to the research questions posed at the end of the paper, which include investigating criteria for sampling the data, investigating data domain adaptation, finding criteria for the useful data abstraction, optimizing the semantic indexing, exploring the semi-supervised learning and active learning.
